useCases.text: As a Regional Marketing Manager, I want to know when and where I should plan my traditional marketing campaigns and field events, with 1–2 weeks heads up. I need Predictive Heatmaps showing where the Crop Window is opening in the next 14 days to prioritize, at district/group of districts/crop region level depending on brand penetration and agronomic profile.

As a Regional Sales Manager, I want to understand how many bottles/liters of a certain product I should plan for which PIN code/subdistrict based on historical data and future weather patterns. Currently basing off subjective info: last year's brand sales, estimated competitor sales from retailer conversations, crop preference changes, sowing window shifts, and general weather. Need to move from subjective to objective — integrating satellite-derived sowing dates and real-time precipitation to predict Inventory Velocity.

As a Sales Head or Crop Manager, I want an overview of what's happening on the ground right now — which of my products should sell now, and where — while sitting in HQ. I need to compare inventory depletion in real time vs ground reality, identify room to sell more, spot new indicators, and decide if inventory needs to move between regions. An Opportunity Gap Analysis tool: if HQ sees high pest pressure in Region A but low inventory or sales, trigger a message to align the sales plan.

As a Field Agent or Marketing Team member, I want to know what to talk about with farmers when visiting — which diseases are occurring right now in the surroundings, which of our products I can recommend, and the benefits vs competitor products. The goal is to create product fit in the farmer's mind based on what's happening in nearby villages or similar crop-profile areas, transitioning from shopkeeper/field agent to trusted advisor.

As a Retailer, I want to be knowledgeable when farmers come into my shop — which diseases are spreading now and which are coming in the next 1–3 weeks, and which products to recommend and why. I need pest trends in the region for same or different crops so I can proactively push the right products. This builds trust and repeat business.

As a Sales or Marketing Head, Business Head, or Crop Manager, I want to plan expansion into new regions and understand the best regions to go to — where are big pockets and TAM for my products. I need to identify regions with high potential and low penetration, knowing that TAM is always dynamic based on crop preference by farmers each season.

As a State Manager selling pre-emergence herbicides, I want to know when farmers put seeds into the ground. I need an automated alert when 20% of the district has completed sowing so my sales team can move herbicides to retail counters immediately — not waiting for delayed manual field reports. Sowing window info is needed as soon as the first 10–20 farmers start sowing for the main season.
As a State Manager selling herbicides, I want to understand which growth stage plants are on the ground right now, because my herbicide sales window depends on precise crop stage timing.
Know how much seed varieties to ship where — unsold stock becomes cow fodder
Understand disease pattern trends and spot new emerging diseases
Know where which crops are planted and likely diseases for Q1/Q2 planning
Get crop acreage and growth stage overview for TAM per subdistrict
Identify patterns of recurring nutrient deficiencies
Know crop stages for next fertilization period
Know when plants have physical stress (bio-stimulant timing)
Understand soil patterns for micronutrients focus areas
Find high density vegetable crop areas (higher spend potential)
Identify crop patterns for specialty product targeting
Get mid-season yield forecasts at district level
Diagnose what disease or pest is affecting my crop from a photo
Get the right treatment recommendation and product dosage for my field size
Know what growth stage my crop is in and what to do next
Get timely alerts about disease outbreaks spreading in my area
Know the best sowing window for my crop and region
Understand weather impact on my crop and plan accordingly
Learn from short educational videos and tips about my specific crop problems
Share disease findings with other farmers in my community via WhatsApp
Get real-time, field-level disease incidence data across multiple states to identify emerging threats and prioritize research
Use disease forecasting models combining weather, historical data, and crop growth stage to anticipate outbreaks 1–2 weeks ahead
Correlate disease pressure data with yield impact to validate product efficacy and build economic justification
Access farmer-submitted photo data to validate crop models, train AI, and improve product recommendations
Use Plantix data for research beyond commercial support — testing formulations, understanding disease ecology, validating models
Efficiently translate technical R&D knowledge into field-usable sales enablement content
Integrate crop growth stage detection with disease data to understand optimal product application timing windows
As a sales head at a seed company, I want to see SRR by crop and region, so that I can identify untapped markets where farmers need seed treatment and direct my sales team accordingly.

useCases.role: Portfolio Manager / Supply Chain / Sales Head
useCases.text: Predict which products to stock in which quantities and regions with a real-time, district-level view of crop acreage, shifting cropping patterns, and pest outbreak probabilities — to avoid chronic over-stocking in wrong geographies and under-stocking during peak demand windows

Know which pests are spreading in which districts in time to advise field staff and adjust stock positioning — current systems aggregate data too slowly and too sparsely, resulting in delayed recommendations and wrong molecules being promoted

Make aligned decisions across teams — sales data lives in SAP, demo outcomes in Excel, liquidation data in field apps, and market intelligence in WhatsApp chats, none connected, resulting in contradictory plans and decisions based on gut feel

Maintain continuity in farmer relationships and territory coverage — field agents collect farmer data in personal notebooks and WhatsApp, so critical contact lists, purchase histories, and relationship networks disappear when staff resign or transfer

Trust liquidation reports from the field team — deadline pressure causes staff to submit estimated or fabricated numbers, resulting in negative liquidation events, product returns, and hours spent on manual cross-verification calls to distributors

Coordinate campaign activities with the sales team — pricing schemes and product placement are finalized by sales without informing marketing, and demo outcomes are not tracked, resulting in misaligned messaging and marketing budgets spent on campaigns that don't match what sales is pushing

Protect seasonal investment from weather and external shocks — narrow spray/sowing windows mean 3–7 days of unseasonal rain or unexpected events can invalidate months of product pre-placement and marketing spend, resulting in large unsold inventory returns and financial write-offs

Maintain healthy cash flow and focus on demand generation — 30–90 day credit cycles with distributors and retailers are routinely extended or defaulted, forcing field teams to act as recovery agents with no time for farmer meetings or product demos

Maintain product differentiation and margin — generic look-alike products priced 30–50% lower crowd the shelf, and retailers switch brands for better credit terms, resulting in brand erosion and shrinking market share

Measure ROI of product demonstrations — demo activities are not systematically recorded from product placement through crop performance to customer conversion, so marketing teams cannot replicate successful demos, justify budgets, or identify which demo conditions drive sales

layers.name: Disease Incidences
layers.notes: Core layer — feeds the most use cases. Dependent on detection model accuracy.

tasks.desc: Aggregate detections by district-crop-week
Normalize by user base to get incidence rate
For maize,rice - add the angle of prevention /curative
For cotton, soybean - mapping  growth stage //sensitivity // treatbility // prevention-curation (for highest impact crop/diseases)

layers.name: Acreage
layers.notes: Combines government data with satellite validation. Key gap: subdistrict granularity. We need to think of any of our solutions to be able to extend this into multiple crops and not manual/different 1:1 (at least major crops/diseases)

tasks.desc: Ingest government crop statistics (state/district)
Validate with satellite imagery (NDVI-based)
Downscale to subdistrict using cropland data
Seasonal update pipeline
Include horticultural data and incorporate datachecks to understand the completenes/validity of the data (start with top crops)
Ask farmers to import their sowing date and the crop they are planning to sow (this can help us on the deltas from season to season)

layers.name: Growth Stage
layers.notes: Currently calendar-based. Photo-based detection being validated.

tasks.desc: Define growth stage calendar per crop-region
Add crop timeline indicators for the crops soybean and cotton
Add growth stage for horticultural crops (tomato, potato, onion, chilli)

layers.name: Sowing Date
layers.notes: Good coverage via detection spike analysis. v1 validated for major crops.

tasks.desc: Establish sowing windows from historical data
Detect sowing date from first-detection spikes
Validate against ground truth surveys

layers.name: Weather
layers.notes: Solid external data source. API pipeline stable.

tasks.desc: Ingest daily weather data via API
Add 7-day forecast integration
Evaluate hyperlocal weather station partnerships

layers.name: Farmer Value
layers.notes: Currently a derived proxy. Needs sales data integration for validation.

tasks.desc: Build basic proxy: acreage × average input spend
Analyse the expenses imported in the calculators in the plantix farmer app - are we able to understand the split between CP/Fertiliser/Herbicides and see any groupings with crop and/or state level?

layers.name: Treatment Recommendations
layers.notes: Well-established. v2 needs field trial data.

tasks.desc: Curate treatment database per disease-crop
Add regional product availability filtering
How do we map now the technicals companies to crop/diseases and dosages? Can we explore ways to add automation in the process? (rob vibe coded something) - focus on labeled claims

layers.name: Demand Forecast
layers.notes: Earliest stage. Depends on Disease Incidences + Acreage + Growth Stage being solid.

tasks.desc: Define demand signal rules (disease spike → product need)
Build demand estimation model
Partner pilot for demand validation

layers.name: Sales Data
layers.notes: Blocked on partner data sharing agreements. Critical for Farmer Value validation.

tasks.desc: CropLife industry sales data: Old 2018 report available by technical × state × crop (INR values plus avg price and quantity at country level); volume-based reports also exist. Newer reports being explored. If Croplife is not possible to look at alternatives
Retailer sales data
Derive the number of treatable acres (acreage x disease x growth stage x treatiblity) - for maize

layers.name: Crop Calendar
layers.notes: Stable foundation. Powers Growth Stage and Sowing Date layers.

tasks.desc: Compile crop calendars from agricultural universities
Adjust for regional climate variations
Auto-update from Plantix detection patterns

layers.name: Farmer Treatment Behavior

versions.label: v0
versions.desc: Uniform assumption — all diseases treated equally

versions.label: v1
versions.desc: Weight by damage score (1-5) per crop-disease

versions.label: v2
versions.desc: Plantix detection prevalence data per geography

layers.notes: Narrows TAM down to SOM — the share that reflects real purchasing behaviour. Not all farmers who face a treatable disease actually spray. Like pharma "treatment rate" — not all diagnosed patients get treated.

tasks.desc: Baseline: uniform treatment assumption across all diseases
Weight by existing damage scores (1-5) for crop-disease combinations
Use % of Plantix images showing specific disease per geography as prevalence
Collect farmer-reported damage level and whether they treated or not
Collect product effectiveness outcomes via in-app surveys
Correlate with commodity prices — cross-validate with LDC back-testing (disease pressure vs yield impact for pulses)
Collect actual yield data and harvest dates via in-app surveys
Collect treatment application timing via in-app surveys (week/growth stage)
Model scouting gap — farmer detection lag affects curative vs preventative TAM
Model preventative spraying patterns (calendar-based or neighbour-driven)
Account for leftover stock usage from previous seasons
Use Plantix popularity data as behavioral signal for farmer awareness/intent
